Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mildly elevated HbA1c, indicating suboptimal long-term glucose control (prediabetes range). Hemoglobin is slightly decreased, suggesting mild anemia. Platelet count and platelet distribution width are elevated, which may indicate reactive thrombocytosis or underlying inflammation. Renal function, electrolytes, and other hematological parameters are within normal limits. Clinical correlation and follow-up are recommended.
